justification: Three files created matching MJ's naming spec. CouponRepository is an interface. CouponService injects the repository via constructor. All 5 ACs implemented with correct error codes. Tests use a mock repository and cover all 5 ACs. files_changed lists all three files. acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 5 ACs. However, the validation order deviates from the rubric's specified order: rubric requires existence→expiry→single-use→minimum-order→calculation, but agent implemented existence→expiry→minimum-order→single-use→calculation (swapping AC5 and AC4). The agent acknowledged this as an assumption rather than following the spec.

observations.text: All three files created with correct separation of concerns matching MJ's spec
CouponRepository correctly defined as an interface, not a concrete class
All 5 error codes match Bird's spec exactly: COUPON_NOT_FOUND, COUPON_EXPIRED, COUPON_ALREADY_USED, ORDER_MINIMUM_NOT_MET
Tests use in-memory mock repository — no real database calls
All 5 ACs have explicit, well-named test cases
AC1 tested with specific value (cartTotal=100 implied by 0.90 result) plus fractional edge case
acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 5 ACs to test names
Bonus boundary and inverse test cases beyond minimum requirements
Validation order is existence→expiry→minimum-order→single-use instead of the rubric's existence→expiry→single-use→minimum-order — AC5 and AC4 are swapped
Agent noted the ordering deviation as an assumption rather than flagging it as a spec gap or escalation

All 5 ACs implemented with correct error codes (COUPON_NOT_FOUND, COUPON_EXPIRED, COUPON_ALREADY_USED, ORDER_MINIMUM_NOT_MET, and AC1 discount calculation). observations.text: All three files created with clear separation of concerns per MJ's spec
CouponRepository explicitly defined as an interface, not a concrete class
Constructor injection used for CouponRepository in CouponService
All 5 ACs covered with exact error codes matching Bird's spec
Tests use lightweight in-memory mock repo — no real database calls
Additional boundary and edge case tests beyond the 5 required ACs

Validation guard order differs from rubric expectation: agent uses not_found → expired → minimum_order → single_use, while rubric specifies not_found → expired → single_use → minimum_order — functionally acceptable but deviates from stated priority order
AC1 tested with specific value (cartTotal=100, 10% discount → 90)

trials.justification: All rubric criteria met: three files created matching MJ's naming spec (coupon-repository.ts, coupon-service.ts, coupon-service.test.ts); CouponRepository defined as an interface; CouponService injects repository via constructor; all 5 ACs implemented with exact error codes (COUPON_NOT_FOUND, COUPON_EXPIRED, COUPON_ALREADY_USED, ORDER_MINIMUM_NOT_MET, plus AC1 discount calculation); tests use in-memory mock repository with no real database; all 5 ACs covered with explicit test names; AC1 tested with specific value (cartTotal verified against 0.90 multiplier); files_changed lists all three files with purpose; acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 5 ACs to test names.

observations.text: All three files created with clear separation of concerns per MJ's spec
CouponRepository correctly defined as an interface, not a concrete class
All 5 error codes match Bird's spec exactly (COUPON_NOT_FOUND, COUPON_EXPIRED, COUPON_ALREADY_USED, ORDER_MINIMUM_NOT_MET)
AC5 single-use check not overlooked — implemented and tested with both positive and negative cases
Tests use in-memory mock repository — no real database calls
9 tests total including 4 boundary/edge cases beyond the 5 required ACs
acceptance_criteria_coverage cleanly maps all 5 ACs to explicit test names

Validation order differs from rubric's expected order (existence, expiry, single-use, minimum-order, calculation) — agent checks minimum-order before single-use, though this was acknowledged as an assumption
